manuscript.title: Reading the Mountain
manuscript.author: Martha Glass
manuscript.synopsis: A popular science book about living with and reading Icelandic volcanic systems, built around the long quiet between eruptions rather than the eruptions themselves.

chapter.heading: The Ground That Does Not Negotiate
chapter.summary: Why a volcano is a long sentence with most of the words missing, not a single dramatic morning.

chapter.heading: Breath of the Mountain
chapter.summary: How gas tells the story before the ground breaks, written for readers who have never held a spectrometer.

chapter.heading: The Mountain That Made Me
chapter.summary: Growing up in Akureyri with Krafla steaming in the kitchen window.

chapter.heading: The Years of Quiet
chapter.summary: The unglamorous work of monitoring, and why the drama everyone photographs is the part we most hope never to need.

chapter.heading: When the Peninsula Woke
chapter.summary: The return of activity after centuries, told from the monitoring side.

chapter.heading: Ice Over Fire
chapter.summary: Reading a system that hides beneath a glacier.

source.note: Eighteenth century eruption records from parish archives
Older Akureyri fishermen who remember weather patterns predating the records

companionSite.note: Monthly field photography and chapter progress notes.

earlyReaderList.note: Short progress note sent each quarter to early readers.
